_comment: Illustrative LLM review output. Actual checklist items and comments will vary.

checklist.comment: Clear and user-centric. Identifies the pain (manual duplication) and scale (time-consuming, error-prone).
Two stories provided, both from the same workflow. Missing: story for admin configuring recurring tasks, story for what happens when a recurring task is missed/overdue.
Three measurable metrics with targets and timeframes. The duplicate detection heuristic needs a definition before engineering builds tracking.
Clear scope-in and scope-out. Exclusion of custom recurrence rules and subtask recurrence prevents scope creep.
Both open questions are still open: deletion behavior and subtask handling. These are blocking questions — they affect DB schema design. Must be resolved before engineering begins.
3-week estimate provided but not validated by engineering. No mention of timezone handling for recurring tasks — this is a common complexity.
Beta plan is reasonable. Missing: rollback plan if beta reveals issues, how beta customers are selected, and what 'GA announcement' means (in-product only, email, etc).
No edge cases documented. Recurring task behavior on: public holidays, team member absence, timezone changes, task reassignment, or archiving a project.

strengths: Clear, quantified success metrics with realistic targets
Explicit scope-out list prevents scope creep
critical_gaps: Open questions on deletion behavior and subtask handling MUST be resolved — these directly impact DB schema
No edge cases documented — at minimum: timezone handling, holiday behavior, and project archiving must be addressed
Duplicate detection heuristic for success metric #2 must be defined
suggested_improvements: Add a user story for the admin/team lead use case
Request engineering to validate the 3-week estimate and confirm timezone handling approach
Define rollback criteria for the beta phase
review.reviewer_summary: This spec has a strong foundation: clear problem, good metrics, and tight scope. However, two critical open questions (deletion behavior and subtask handling) must be resolved before engineering begins, as they directly affect database schema design. The edge case section is missing entirely. Recommend addressing critical gaps, then re-submitting for a quick second pass.
